description_long: This object is a classic CRT computer monitor with a boxy, deep chassis and a slightly curved glass display. The front features a thick, matte gray bezel surrounding the screen, with a row of small, round control buttons and input jacks located along the lower edge. The sides and rear are constructed from black plastic, with the back panel extending outward in a pronounced, angular bulge typical of cathode ray tube technology. Ventilation grilles are visible on the rear and top surfaces, and a label with technical information is affixed to the back. The monitor sits on a flat, integrated base that is flush with the bottom of the unit, providing stability but no visible adjustability.
description: A bulky, retro CRT computer monitor with a thick gray bezel, deep black rear housing, and front-facing control buttons.
description_view_0: Front view showing the curved glass screen, thick gray bezel, and a row of control buttons and input jacks along the bottom edge.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the monitor's deep, protruding black rear housing and the flat, gray side panel.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the large, vented black back panel, technical label, and the monitor's integrated base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the depth of the rear housing and the flat side panel.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, flat, and matte gray, with squared corners and a smooth, untextured finish. It frames the display area with a uniform width and has no decorative elements.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor has an integrated flat base that is flush with the bottom, made of the same plastic as the housing, with no visible adjustability or separate pedestal.
attribute_button_placement: A row of small, round buttons is located along the bottom edge of the front bezel, grouped together near the center.
